zh.Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾卡里克
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway於1126年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾卡里克時，他的父親Fergus of Galloway46歲，母親Elizabeth FitzRoy31歲。他和Eve mac Fergus至少有1個兒子。他去世於1185年1月1日在他的家鄉享年59歲。

zh-hans.Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland: 英国苏格兰艾斯爾卡里克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway于1126年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾卡里克时，他的父亲Fergus of Galloway46岁，母亲Elizabeth FitzRoy31岁。他和Eve mac Fergus至少有1个儿子。他去世于1185年1月1日在他的家乡享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Cuong, Minh, Lien, Muoi, Thanh, Tho, Tieu, Vinh, Anh, Beo, Binh, Chau.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 麥 (see   Mai   2) and 莫 (see   Mo   1).
Vietnamese (Mạc): from Chinese 莫 ‘nothing; nonexistent’; see Mo 1.

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Fergusa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, formed with fear ‘man’ + gus ‘vigor, force’. This was the name of an early Irish mythological figure, a valiant warrior, also of the grandfather of Saint Columba.
Scottish and English: from the Gaelic personal name Fearghus (see 1 above).

variants.name: Ferries
meanings: Scottish: according to Black, either a shortened form of MacFerries, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fearghuis ‘son of Fergus ’, or an altered form of Fergus itself. Compare Ferris .
Irish and Scottish: variant of   Ferris  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name, a variant of   Ferris  .

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferguson
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fergus ‘son of Fearghus’ (see Fergus ) by substituting -son for mac-.

variants.name: Farrish
meanings: Scottish and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’ (see Fergus , and compare Ferris , Farris ).

variants.name: Farris
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of Fergus , in which the Gaelic gh sound has been dropped rather than being altered to g. Compare Farrish , Ferris .
Probably also English: variant of   Farrar  . The name is quite common in southeastern England.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Galloway
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Galloway in southwest Scotland, named as ‘place of the foreign Gaels’, from Gaelic gall ‘foreigner’ + Gaidheal ‘Gael’. From the 8th century or before it was a province of Anglian Northumbria. In the 9th century it was settled by mixed Gaelic-Norse inhabitants from the Hebrides and Isle of Man.

variants.name: Gallaway
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Galloway  .

variants.name: Galway
meanings: Irish and Scottish: in most cases, probably a variant of Scottish   Galloway  , rather than from the city or county in Connacht (habitational names are rare in Ireland). During the Middle Ages Galways were wealthy merchants on the south coast of Ireland, but now the name there is largely confined to Ulster.

nameMeanings.name: Gille
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Aegidus, Old French Gide. See Giles .

nameMeanings.name: Brigte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of   Brighid .

variants.name: Brie
meanings: Variant spelling of Bree , altered in form to coincide with the name of a district in northern France (so called from Late Latin bracia ‘marshland’) famous for its production of a variety of soft cheese. This is now a relatively common first name in Australia.

variants.name: Bree
meanings: Anglicized form of Irish   Brighe   .

en.lifeSummary: When Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway was born in 1126, in Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Fergus of Galloway, was 46 and his mother, Elizabeth FitzRoy, was 31. He had at least 1 son with Eve mac Fergus. He died on 1 January 1185, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway est né en 1126, à Carrick, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Fergus of Galloway, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth FitzRoy, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Eve mac Fergus. Il est décédé le 1 janvier 1185, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway 1126 in Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fergus of Galloway 46 und seine Mutter Elizabeth FitzRoy 31. Er hatte mit Eve mac Fergus mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 1. Januar 1185 im Alter von 59 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway è nato il nel 1126 nel Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Fergus of Galloway, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth FitzRoy, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Eve mac Fergus. Morì il 1 gennaio 1185, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Gallowayが1126年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアカーリックで生まれた時，父Fergus of Gallowayは46歳，母Elizabeth FitzRoyは31歳であった。彼はEve mac Fergusとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1185年1月1日に故郷で59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway(이)가 1126년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 카릭에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fergus of Galloway(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth FitzRoy(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Eve mac Fergus(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1185년 1월 1일 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway nasceu em 1126, em Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Fergus of Galloway, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth FitzRoy, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Eve mac Fergus. Ele faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1185, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway родился в 1126 г. в местности каррикк, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Fergus of Galloway было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth FitzRoy, 31. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Eve mac Fergus. Он умер 1 января 1185 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway nació en 1126, en Carrick, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Fergus of Galloway, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Elizabeth FitzRoy, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Eve mac Fergus. Murió el 1 de enero de 1185, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
